name: Iterative In-Place Pointer Reversal
summary: Walk through the list once, reversing each next pointer so every processed node points backward.
visualizationIntent: init: show the original linked list with previous before the head and current at the head, loop: highlight current as the node being processed, move: save current's original next pointer in nxt before breaking the arrow, update: redirect current's next arrow to previous and then move previous/current one step forward, return: traverse from previous, the new head, and show the reversed values.

lineExplanations.3: Start with an empty result list for the final reversed values.
lineExplanations.4: Copy the input values into node storage.
lineExplanations.6: Create the next-pointer array, with every node initially ending at -1.
lineExplanations.7: Start at the first index while building the original list links.
lineExplanations.8: Continue while node i should point to another node after it.
lineExplanations.9: Set node i's next pointer to node i + 1.
lineExplanations.10: Advance i to build the next original link.
lineExplanations.11: Set previous to -1 because no node comes before the original head.
lineExplanations.12: Start current at the original head node.
lineExplanations.13: Check whether the list is empty.
lineExplanations.14: For an empty list, set current to -1 so no reversal loop runs.
lineExplanations.15: Initialize nxt, the saved original next pointer.
lineExplanations.16: Start with no changed links highlighted.
lineExplanations.17: Continue reversing while current points to a real node.
lineExplanations.18: Save current's original next pointer before overwriting it.
lineExplanations.19: Mark current as the node whose link will change.
lineExplanations.20: Reverse current's next pointer so it points back to previous.
lineExplanations.21: Move previous forward to current because current is now the front of the reversed part.
lineExplanations.22: Move current to the saved original next node.
lineExplanations.23: Clear changed-link highlighting before reading the answer.
lineExplanations.24: Set current to previous, which is the new head after reversal.
lineExplanations.25: Walk through the reversed list to collect values.
lineExplanations.26: Read the value at the current node.
lineExplanations.27: Append the current value to the output list.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def reverseList(self, head): defines the method; head is the input list of linked-list values.
syntaxExplanations.3: result = [] creates an empty Python list.
syntaxExplanations.5: len(nodes) returns the number of nodes.
syntaxExplanations.6: [-1] * n creates a list with n copies of -1.
syntaxExplanations.7: i = 0 assigns the first index to i.
syntaxExplanations.8: while i < n - 1: repeats while i is before the last index.
syntaxExplanations.9: links[i] = i + 1 writes into the links list at position i.
syntaxExplanations.12: current = 0 stores the head node ID for non-empty lists.
syntaxExplanations.13: if n == 0: checks whether there are no nodes.
syntaxExplanations.14: current = -1 replaces current with the no-node sentinel.
syntaxExplanations.16: changed_links = [] creates an empty list of changed link IDs.
syntaxExplanations.17: while current != -1: repeats while current is a valid node ID.
syntaxExplanations.18: nxt = links[current] reads the next pointer of current.
syntaxExplanations.19: changed_links = [current] creates a one-item list containing current.
syntaxExplanations.20: links[current] = previous rewrites current's next pointer.
syntaxExplanations.21: previous = current assigns current's node ID to previous.
syntaxExplanations.22: current = nxt moves current to the saved next node.
syntaxExplanations.24: current = previous starts traversal from the new head.
syntaxExplanations.25: while current != -1: loops through the reversed list.
syntaxExplanations.26: value = nodes[current] reads the node value at current.
syntaxExplanations.27: result.append(value) adds value to the end of result.
syntaxExplanations.28: current = links[current] follows the reversed next pointer.
syntaxExplanations.29: return result sends the final answer back to the caller.
